methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 20 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Ukrspecsystems is a growth-stage defense and security company founded by Kostyuk Vyacheslav Nikolaevich, operating in a sector with a modest comparable universe of 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries.

Traditional peer-median valuation does not apply here. The narrow-filter peer-matching approach yielded insufficient datapoints: zero same-stage comparables, one adjacent-stage comparable, and zero similar-tier peers within the Defense & Security sector. This scarcity reflects the heterogeneous nature of defense tech valuations, where company scale, contract structure, and technical differentiation diverge sharply across the stage spectrum. A broad all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise and misrepresent the company's position.

Given these constraints, no triangulated valuation range can be reliably derived from the available data. Credible valuation of Ukrspecsystems would require either: (1) transaction multiples from recent defense-tech financings with disclosed terms; (2) discounted cash flow analysis anchored to contract pipeline and margin assumptions; or (3) expansion of the peer set to adjacent subsectors with sufficient comparable depth. The absence of quantitative anchors underscores that valuation here must rest on company-specific fundamentals—contract revenue visibility, technical defensibility, and capital efficiency—rather than market multiples.
